passage_text: As used in this article, unless the context otherwise requires: (2) 'Consequential decision' means a decision that has a material legal or similarly significant effect on the provision or denial to any consumer of: (a) Education enrollment or an education opportunity; (b) Employment or an employment opportunity; (c) A financial or lending service; (d) An essential government service; (e) Health-care services; (f) Housing; (g) Insurance; or (h) A legal service.

definition.definition_text: a decision that has a material legal or similarly significant effect on the provision or denial to any consumer of: education enrollment, employment, financial or lending service, essential government service, health-care services, housing, insurance, or a legal service

ambiguity.interpretation_notes: The qualifier 'similarly significant' alongside 'material legal' creates uncertainty about what non-legal effects qualify as consequential
ambiguity.suggested_clarification: Provide examples or criteria for what constitutes a 'similarly significant' non-legal effect
